questions.q: The Reservation buying type is available only for:
questions.explanation: Reservation = predictable delivery and price, limited to Awareness and Engagement. Everything else runs on Auction.

questions.q: The auction's total value is made up of:
options: Advertiser bid + estimated action rates + ad quality
questions.explanation: Total value = advertiser bid + estimated action rates + ad quality. The bid strategy/cost control does NOT enter.

options: Highest Volume, Highest Value, Cost per Result Goal, ROAS Goal, Bid Cap
Daily, Lifetime, CBO, ABO, Bid Cap
questions.explanation: Spend-based (Highest Volume/Value), goal-based (Cost per Result/ROAS Goal) and manual (Bid Cap).

questions.q: A client with a strict CPA ceiling that optimizes for number of results:
questions.explanation: Cost per Result Goal maintains a target cost per result while seeking volume. Highest Volume doesn't respect a cost ceiling.

questions.q: When is Reservation preferable to Auction?
options: When you want the lowest possible cost
When you need guaranteed reach/frequency at a fixed CPM and planning in advance
questions.explanation: Reservation gives predictability: fixed delivery and price. It isn't necessarily cheaper nor does it support Sales.
